what_it_does: Executes acceptance checks against runtime.session_supervisor: it asserts the spawn command builder produces a byte-identical default and threads each new optional parameter into the claude argv; validates the SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY registry rows and _apply_spawn_flags posture/consent/refusal behavior; tests _extract_usage cost/token extraction; and runs a self-contained stub-claude end-to-end supervisor to confirm usage blocks reach events.jsonl.
observations: The default built command is compared exactly to a hard-coded EXPECTED_DEFAULT to enforce byte-identical pre-widening behavior.
Each new optional spawn parameter (model, effort, fallback, permission_mode, settings, add_dir, output_format, include_partial, debug, safe_mode, bare) is mapped to a specific claude CLI flag.
SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY rows are checked for flag, kind, teach, and a real registry-derived posture; the old hand-maintained SPAWN_FLAGS dict is asserted gone.
_apply_spawn_flags is tested for no-op, safe value/bool/csv/repeat emission, swap-in-place for consent rows, TeachingRefusal for unknown keys, and locked-path refusals.
_extract_usage is exercised with full usage, cost-only, alternate cost_usd spelling, model inference from modelUsage, and the no-report None case.
An end-to-end test launches the supervisor as a subprocess with a stub claude executable, calls /spawn and /inject over HTTP, then polls events.jsonl for an agent_sessions.turn event carrying usage.
standouts: A stub-claude subprocess integration test verifies durable turn usage capture in events.jsonl without a real model.
The byte-identical default command invariant is treated as the hardest guarantee.
Posture is asserted to be derived from a registry rather than from a hand-coded SPAWN_FLAGS table.
conventions: Self-contained runnable script with __main__ guard and sys.exit on AssertionError.
Small helper functions (check, build, pair_after, apply, _req) isolate repetitive assertion patterns.
Environment isolation by popping COMPANY_FABRIC_PERMISSION and overriding env vars for the subprocess test.
concerns: The end-to-end test hardcodes port 8783 and uses polling/sleep loops, making it sensitive to port collisions and timing.
It assumes a .venv/bin/python path and falls back to sys.executable.
main mixes unit assertions, registry validation, and integration testing in one long function.
notes: A runnable acceptance harness that treats spawn command construction, usage extraction, and registry-based flag assembly as executable specifications.
questions: What is the exact schema of SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY and the _registry_posture implementation in runtime/session_supervisor.py?
How does the supervisor write agent_sessions.turn events to events.jsonl and what fields are guaranteed?
What should happen when multiple consent-class flags are requested without consent?
Is settings expected to be a JSON string, a file path, or both?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it unit-tests cost/usage capture and spawn parameter widening, and uses a stub claude for the end-to-end coverage claim rather than a real model.
apparent_themes: Byte-identical default behavior under parameter widening
Posture-based safe/consent/locked gating of CLI flags
intention_signals: To lock down that adding new spawn parameters does not change the default command, that result-event cost and usage are preserved end-to-end, and that flag assembly is governed by a registry-derived posture model with clear refusal paths.
novelty: Combines fine-grained command-builder unit assertions with a self-contained stub-claude subprocess integration test in a single runnable script.
contribution: Provides an executable, file-local specification of spawn command construction, usage extraction, registry-based flag assembly rules, and a stub-claude end-to-end check that records turn usage in events.jsonl.
summary_for_embedding: A runnable acceptance test for session_supervisor that verifies spawn command parameter widening, registry-derived flag assembly posture rules, usage/cost extraction, and a stub-claude end-to-end events.jsonl turn usage capture.
intention_for_embedding: To guarantee that new optional spawn parameters leave default behavior byte-identical, that result-event usage and cost are captured durably, and that flag assembly is governed by a registry-derived posture model.

symbols.does: Counts and prints passing assertions, raising AssertionError on failure.
symbols.description: Test assertion helper used throughout main; it increments a global pass counter and prints an ok line, or fails the whole run with a labeled message.

symbols.does: Builds a spawn command via SessionSupervisor._build_spawn_cmd with given kwargs.
symbols.description: Wraps the supervisor's private command builder, supplying the always-present claude_bin, resume, and fork defaults and merging any widened parameter overrides so tests can inspect the resulting argv list.

symbols.does: Returns the argv token immediately following a given flag.
symbols.description: Small helper for reading key/value pairs from the built command list; used to assert that flags like --model or --permission-mode are followed by the expected value.

symbols.does: Runs all Family 1 and Family 2 acceptance checks.
symbols.description: Top-level test driver that exercises default/widened spawn command construction, SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY registry validation, _apply_spawn_flags posture/consent behavior, _extract_usage unit cases, and a stub-claude end-to-end supervisor run.

symbols.does: Applies spawn flags to a base command via _apply_spawn_flags.
symbols.description: Nested helper that defaults to the standard built command and forwards a flags dict and optional consent flag to the supervisor's flag-application routine.

symbols.does: Makes an HTTP request to the stub supervisor during the end-to-end test.
symbols.description: Nested helper used in the integration phase to call /health, /spawn, and /inject with JSON bodies and return status plus parsed response.
